observations: Ground-mounted commercial/utility-scale solar array visible — multiple rows of large panel strings on fixed-tilt racking structures across an open paddock.
Array appears to be a multi-row ground mount installation, likely in a rural or semi-rural Australian setting.
No workers, PPE, tools, or active install activity are visible in this frame — site appears unoccupied or worker is off-frame.
Sky shows significant cumulus cloud build-up — weather conditions should be monitored for wind and lightning risk.

installQualityFlags.message: No workers visible — cannot assess fall protection, PPE, or active workmanship. Ensure all personnel working on or around the racking are wearing high-vis and appropriate PPE per site safe work method statement.

Heavy cumulus cloud build-up visible overhead. Monitor Bureau of Meteorology for storm, high wind, or lightning warnings. Ground-mount sites are highly exposed — suspend work and move personnel to shelter if thunderstorm approaches within 10 km.

Panel rows in mid-field appear to have minor angular variation visible at distance — walk each row and verify all panels are clamped flush to rails with no tilt or lift at corners before string commissioning.

DC cable routing beneath racking is not visible from this angle — verify all inter-string cables are clipped to racking with UV-rated ties at no more than 300 mm intervals and are not resting on bare ground.

timeOnTaskNote: No active worker visible in frame. If the team is on a break or off-site, re-assess schedule against remaining row count. Ground-mount arrays of this scale typically require tight sequencing between mechanical install, cable routing, and string testing to hit end-of-day targets.
nextSteps: Walk the full length of each panel row and physically check that every mid-clamp and end-clamp is torqued to the manufacturer specification — use a calibrated torque wrench and mark each bolt with a torque marker pen.
Inspect all DC cable runs beneath the racking — cables must be secured to racking members, not lying on soil, to prevent UV degradation and vermin damage.
Check BOM weather radar now — if storm cells are developing within 30 km, secure any loose tools or materials and brief the team on shelter and stop-work protocol.

visualCallouts.label: Storm cloud risk
visualCallouts.message: Heavy cloud building overhead — check BOM radar for lightning and stop work if storm is within 10 kilometres.

visualCallouts.label: Inspect cable routing
visualCallouts.message: Check DC cables under the racking are clipped off the ground and secured with UV-rated ties every 300 millimetres.

spokenCue.say: Check BOM radar now — storm clouds are building overhead.
